TEXT.citation: Wikipedia contributors. (2026). Sakizaya Wikipedia. Wikimedia Foundation. Retrieved 2026-06-09, from https://szy.wikipedia.org/
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @misc{Wiki_szy, title = {Sakizaya Wikipedia}, author = {{Wikipedia contributors}}, year = {2026}, publisher = {Wikimedia Foundation}, url = {https://szy.wikipedia.org/}, urldate = {2026-06-09} }

S.FORM: == balidas == 3m ku takalaw, apuyu' ku kaway. sibalu i sadingsing. takuliwaya ku heci. cay katalabu tu lala' ku lamit. madicem ku papah. u siwkay nu nipaluma pasubana i cacudadan a lacul u cudad a ngangan: arenga tremula blanco becc. u sapamat a ngangan: palmae u Amilika a ngangan: formosan sugar palm, Taiwan sugarpalm, areng, arenga u zuma a cidekay a kamu Pangcha: x Tayan: x Sejek: x Paywan: 'legis Taluku: x Yuwatan: asik Rukay: langusadre Cou: taruvuku Kabalan: x Kanakanabu: x Laaluwa: x Saysia: x Puyuma: 'atamur Saw: x Tau: x u sulit nu zuma a kamu u sulit nu Hulam ŚĪĪś£ē sancong u sulit nu Amilika Arenga engleri, or the Formosa palm, Taiwan sugar palm, dwarf sugar palm, or Taiwan arenga palm, is a species of flowering plant in the Arecaceae family. The plant rarely grows more than 10 ft. tall, with a stem diameter of 6in. and a spread of 16 ft. The palm is native to Taiwan as well as Japan's Ryukyu Islands. The fruit of the palm is known to cause a severe allergic reaction. saungay misapayu' a cidekay Yuwatan, Sakizaya, Cou u zuma a cidekay cay katineng mukan atu musapayu' tina nipaluma
S.PHON: == bar~…ĺidas == *m ku takar~…ĺaw, apuju Ē~ ° ku kawaj. sibar~…ĺu i sadiŇčsiŇč. takur~…ĺiwaja ku ńß…ô ¶i. ¶aj katar~…ĺabu tu r~…ĺar~…ĺa Ē~ ° ku r~…ĺamit. madi ¶…ôm ku papańß. u siwkaj nu nipar~…ĺuma pasubana i ¶a ¶udadan a r~…ĺa ¶ur~…ĺ u ¶udad a ŇčaŇčan: ar…ôŇča tr…ômur~…ĺa br~…ĺan ¶o b…ô ¶ ¶. u sapamat a ŇčaŇčan: par~…ĺma…ô u amir~…ĺika a ŇčaŇčan: formosan sugar par~…ĺm, taiwan sugarpar~…ĺm, ar…ôŇč, ar…ôŇča u zuma a ¶id…ôkaj a kamu paŇč ¶ńßa: * tajan: * s…ôj…ôk: * pajwan: Ē~ °r~…ĺ…ôgis tar~…ĺuku: * juwatan: asik *ukaj: r~…ĺaŇčusadr…ô ¶ou: taru*uku kabar~…ĺan: * kanakanabu: * r~…ĺaar~…ĺuwa: * sajsia: * pujuma: Ē~ °atamur saw: * tau: * u sur~…ĺit nu zuma a kamu u sur~…ĺit nu ńßur~…ĺam ** san ¶oŇč u sur~…ĺit nu amir~…ĺika ar…ôŇča …ôŇčr~…ĺ…ôri, or tńß…ô formosa par~…ĺm, taiwan sugar par~…ĺm, dwarf sugar par~…ĺm, or taiwan ar…ôŇča par~…ĺm, is a sp…ô ¶i…ôs of fr~…ĺow…ôriŇč pr~…ĺant in tńß…ô ar…ô ¶a ¶…ôa…ô famir~…ĺj. tńß…ô pr~…ĺant rar…ôr~…ĺj grows mor…ô tńßan ** ft. tar~…ĺr~…ĺ, witńß a st…ôm diam…ôt…ôr of *in. and a spr…ôad of ** ft. tńß…ô par~…ĺm is nati*…ô to taiwan as w…ôr~…ĺr~…ĺ as *apan Ē~ °s *jukju isr~…ĺands. tńß…ô fruit of tńß…ô par~…ĺm is known to ¶aus…ô a s…ô*…ôr…ô ar~…ĺr~…ĺ…ôrgi ¶ r…ôa ¶tion. sauŇčaj misapaju Ē~ ° a ¶id…ôkaj juwatan, sakizaja, ¶ou u zuma a ¶id…ôkaj ¶aj katin…ôŇč mukan atu musapaju Ē~ ° tina nipar~…ĺuma
S.FORM: == balidas == 3m ku takalaw, apuyu' ku kaway. sibalu i sadingsing. takuliwaya ku heci. cay katalabu tu lala' ku lamit. madicem ku papah. u siwkay nu nipaluma pasubana i cacudadan a lacul u cudad a ngangan: arenga tremula blanco becc. u sapamat a ngangan: palmae u Amilika a ngangan: formosan sugar palm, Taiwan sugarpalm, areng, arenga u zuma a cidekay a kamu Pangcha: x Tayan: x Sejek: x Paywan: 'legis Taluku: x Yuwatan: asik Rukay: langusadre Cou: taruvuku Kabalan: x Kanakanabu: x Laaluwa: x Saysia: x Puyuma: 'atamur Saw: x Tau: x u sulit nu zuma a kamu u sulit nu Hulam ŚĪĪś£ē sancong u sulit nu Amilika Arenga engleri, or the Formosa palm, Taiwan sugar palm, dwarf sugar palm, or Taiwan arenga palm, is a species of flowering plant in the Arecaceae family. The plant rarely grows more than 10 ft. tall, with a stem diameter of 6in. and a spread of 16 ft. The palm is native to Taiwan as well as Japan's Ryukyu Islands. The fruit of the palm is known to cause a severe allergic reaction. saungay misapayu' a cidekay Yuwatan, Sakizaya, Cou u zuma a cidekay cay katineng mukan atu musapayu' tina nipaluma
S.PHON: == bar~…ĺidas == *m ku takar~…ĺaw, apuju Ē~ ° ku kawaj. sibar~…ĺu i sadiŇčsiŇč. takur~…ĺiwaja ku ńß…ô ¶i. ¶aj katar~…ĺabu tu r~…ĺar~…ĺa Ē~ ° ku r~…ĺamit. madi ¶…ôm ku papańß. u siwkaj nu nipar~…ĺuma pasubana i ¶a ¶udadan a r~…ĺa ¶ur~…ĺ u ¶udad a ŇčaŇčan: ar…ôŇča tr…ômur~…ĺa br~…ĺan ¶o b…ô ¶ ¶. u sapamat a ŇčaŇčan: par~…ĺma…ô u amir~…ĺika a ŇčaŇčan: formosan sugar par~…ĺm, taiwan sugarpar~…ĺm, ar…ôŇč, ar…ôŇča u zuma a ¶id…ôkaj a kamu paŇč ¶ńßa: * tajan: * s…ôj…ôk: * pajwan: Ē~ °r~…ĺ…ôgis tar~…ĺuku: * juwatan: asik *ukaj: r~…ĺaŇčusadr…ô ¶ou: taru*uku kabar~…ĺan: * kanakanabu: * r~…ĺaar~…ĺuwa: * sajsia: * pujuma: Ē~ °atamur saw: * tau: * u sur~…ĺit nu zuma a kamu u sur~…ĺit nu ńßur~…ĺam ** san ¶oŇč u sur~…ĺit nu amir~…ĺika ar…ôŇča …ôŇčr~…ĺ…ôri, or tńß…ô formosa par~…ĺm, taiwan sugar par~…ĺm, dwarf sugar par~…ĺm, or taiwan ar…ôŇča par~…ĺm, is a sp…ô ¶i…ôs of fr~…ĺow…ôriŇč pr~…ĺant in tńß…ô ar…ô ¶a ¶…ôa…ô famir~…ĺj. tńß…ô pr~…ĺant rar…ôr~…ĺj grows mor…ô tńßan ** ft. tar~…ĺr~…ĺ, witńß a st…ôm diam…ôt…ôr of *in. and a spr…ôad of ** ft. tńß…ô par~…ĺm is nati*…ô to taiwan as w…ôr~…ĺr~…ĺ as *apan Ē~ °s *jukju isr~…ĺands. tńß…ô fruit of tńß…ô par~…ĺm is known to ¶aus…ô a s…ô*…ôr…ô ar~…ĺr~…ĺ…ôrgi ¶ r…ôa ¶tion. sauŇčaj misapaju Ē~ ° a ¶id…ôkaj juwatan, sakizaja, ¶ou u zuma a ¶id…ôkaj ¶aj katin…ôŇč mukan atu musapaju Ē~ ° tina nipar~…ĺuma
